title: Health Vocabulary
question: What do you call a pain in your head?
explanation: A headache is the specific word for pain felt in the head.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: If your body temperature is very high, you have a _____.
explanation: A fever means your body temperature is higher than normal, usually because of illness.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: You go to a pharmacy to buy _____.
explanation: A pharmacy is a shop that sells medicine and health products.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: I need to make an _____ with the doctor.
explanation: You make an appointment to schedule a time to see a doctor.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: The doctor wrote me a _____ for antibiotics.
explanation: A prescription is a doctor's written order for medicine.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: A person who looks after patients in a hospital is a _____.
explanation: A nurse is a trained medical professional who cares for patients.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: I have a sore _____. It hurts when I swallow.
explanation: Your throat is the passage at the back of your mouth. A sore throat makes swallowing painful.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: She _____ her ankle while running.
explanation: To sprain means to injure a joint by twisting it. Ankles are commonly sprained.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: You should _____ if you feel very sick.
explanation: When you feel very sick, the best advice is to see a doctor for help.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: He has a bad _____. He keeps coughing all day.
explanation: A cold is a common illness that causes coughing, sneezing, and a runny nose.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: The dentist checks your _____.
explanation: A dentist is a doctor who specializes in caring for teeth.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: Which word describes feeling unsteady, as if the room is spinning around you?
explanation: Dizzy means feeling unsteady or like the room is spinning. Thirsty means needing a drink, bored means uninterested, lonely means without company.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: Which item do you put on a cut to cover and protect the wound?
explanation: A bandage is a strip of material for covering wounds. A blanket is for a bed, a belt holds up trousers, a button fastens clothes.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: She has an _____ to peanuts. She cannot eat them.
explanation: An allergy is a harmful reaction your body has to certain foods or substances.

title: Health Vocabulary
question: I cut my finger and it started to _____.
explanation: When you cut yourself, blood comes out. This is called bleeding.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is it when the clock shows 3:15?
explanation: Quarter past means 15 minutes after the hour.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 7:30?
explanation: Half past means 30 minutes after the hour.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 4:55?
explanation: Five to five means 5 minutes before 5 o'clock, which is 4:55.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 9:45?
explanation: Quarter to means 15 minutes before the next hour. 9:45 is quarter to ten.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 6:05?
explanation: Five past means 5 minutes after the hour.

title: Telling Time
question: It is 2:30. Which is correct?
explanation: 2:30 is 30 minutes past 2, so it is half past two.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 11:15?
explanation: Quarter past means 15 minutes after the hour. 11:15 is quarter past eleven.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 8:50?
explanation: Ten to nine means 10 minutes before 9 o'clock, which is 8:50.

title: Telling Time
question: Which word means 12:00 in the middle of the day?
explanation: Noon is 12:00 in the middle of the day. Midnight is 12:00 at night. Half past twelve is 12:30. Quarter to twelve is 11:45.

title: Telling Time
question: What time is 1:40?
explanation: Twenty to two means 20 minutes before 2 o'clock, which is 1:40.

title: Daily Routines
question: Which phrasal verb means to stop sleeping in the morning?
explanation: Wake up means to stop sleeping. Give up means to stop trying, look up means to search for information, pick up means to lift something.

title: Daily Routines
question: After my shower, I _____ and choose my clothes.
explanation: Get dressed means to put on your clothes.

title: Daily Routines
question: She _____ her teeth twice a day.
explanation: We use the verb brush with teeth. You brush your teeth with a toothbrush.

title: Daily Routines
question: I _____ to work by train every day.
explanation: To commute means to travel regularly between your home and your workplace.

title: Daily Routines
question: He _____ breakfast at 8 o'clock.
explanation: We say have breakfast (or has for he/she). This means to eat breakfast.

title: Daily Routines
question: Which verb do we use with 'a book' to mean looking at the words and understanding them?
explanation: We read a book. Ride is for bikes and horses, drive is for cars, drink is for liquids.

title: Daily Routines
question: She _____ a shower every morning before work.
explanation: In English, we say take a shower. This is a fixed expression.

title: Daily Routines
question: I _____ to bed at 10 p.m. on weekdays.
explanation: We say go to bed when we mean going to sleep at night.

title: Daily Routines
question: He _____ home from work at around 6 p.m.
explanation: Get home means to arrive at your home. He gets home at 6 p.m.

title: Daily Routines
question: Which verb is used in the fixed expression meaning to tidy the sheets and covers on a bed in the morning?
explanation: The fixed expression is 'make the bed'. We do not say 'do the bed', 'have the bed', or 'take the bed' for this meaning.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: I _____ go to the gym. I go five times a week.
explanation: Often means many times or frequently. Going five times a week is very frequent.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: She _____ eats fast food. She prefers healthy meals.
explanation: Rarely means almost never. If she prefers healthy meals, she almost never eats fast food.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: He _____ drinks coffee. He has a cup every single morning.
explanation: Always means every time, without exception. Every single morning means always.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: We _____ go swimming — maybe once or twice a month.
explanation: Sometimes means occasionally, not very often. Once or twice a month is occasional.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: I exercise three times _____.
explanation: A week is the correct time period here. Three times a week is a common frequency expression.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: She _____ takes the bus. She drives to work every day without exception.
explanation: Never means not at any time. If she drives every day without exception, she never takes the bus.

title: Frequency Expressions
question: How _____ do you visit the dentist?
explanation: How often asks about frequency — how many times something happens in a period.

title: Health Advice
question: Which drink is recommended when you are sick to keep your body hydrated?
explanation: Water hydrates the body. Coffee can dehydrate you, and beer and wine are alcohol, which is not recommended when sick.

title: Health Advice
question: Which word means plant foods like carrots, broccoli, and spinach?
explanation: Vegetables are plant foods like carrots and broccoli. Vegetarians are people who don't eat meat; 'vegetations' and 'vegetables's' are not correct English word forms.

title: Health Advice
question: Which word means stopping activity so your body can recover when you are ill?
explanation: Rest means to stop activity and relax so the body recovers. Run, jump, and dance are active movements that tire the body.

title: Health Advice
question: Which verb means to clean something with water and soap?
explanation: Wash means to clean with water and soap. Shake, wave, and point are hand movements that do not clean anything.

title: Health Advice
question: Which word means the rest your body gets at night with your eyes closed?
explanation: Sleep is the state of rest at night. Food is what you eat, work is your job, exercise is physical activity.

title: Health Advice
question: Which word means physical activity like running or lifting weights that keeps your body fit?
explanation: Exercise is physical activity for fitness. Medicine treats illness, furniture is tables and chairs, homework is school tasks.

title: Health Advice
question: Which verb means to not eat a meal (for example, not eating breakfast)?
explanation: To skip a meal means to not eat it. Cook means to prepare food, chew means to grind food with your teeth, swallow means to send food down your throat.

title: Health Advice
question: Smoking is very bad for your _____.
explanation: Smoking damages your body and is very harmful to your health.
